data.title: Brainstorm gap: config validation behavior for remediate thresholds unspecified
data.description:
Epic w21-bsnz (remediate legacy code) scenario analysis identified three configuration failure modes but brainstorm did not resolve the defensive behavior:

1. auto_threshold=0 — routes every match to auto-prune, bypassing blue-team entirely (rated critical)
2. blue_team_threshold > auto_threshold — creates a dead zone where routing is undefined (rated important)
3. Missing config keys — bash numeric comparison against empty string aborts silently (rated important)

The spec defines the two config keys and their defaults but says nothing about what happens on invalid values. This is a user-facing behavioral decision:
- Option A: Reject invalid config at skill startup with an error message
- Option B: Clamp to safe range (e.g., auto >= blue_team, both in 0-100)
- Option C: Warn and fall back to defaults

Impact: Without a decision, implementation will pick arbitrarily and may not handle all three cases consistently.

Resolution: Clarify user intent on validation behavior and add to SC2 before preplanning.
